Each row → exactly one phase+repo (tier-respecting → counts reconcile to 142 core / 85 deferred / 227). formal_name is a VALID, UNIQUE planned Rocq identifier (finalized at implementation). status_semantics gives the exact settled/open proposition for every non-open record. Provenance: graph-conjectures@f6901fb371, data/problems.json@27aec7fadb. phase_basis records how the phase was chosen.

rows.title: 2-colouring a graph without a monochromatic maximum clique
rows.source_text: Conjecture If $ G $ is a non-empty graph containing no induced odd cycle of length at least $ 5 $ , then there is a $ 2 $ -vertex colouring of $ G $ in which no maximum clique is monochromatic.

source_propositions.text: If $ G $ is a non-empty graph containing no induced odd cycle of length at least $ 5 $ , then there is a $ 2 $ -vertex colouring of $ G $ in which no maximum clique is monochromatic.

rows.title: Book Thickness of Subdivisions
rows.source_text: Let $ G $ be a finite undirected simple graph. A $ k $ -page book embedding of $ G $ consists of a linear order $ \preceq $ of $ V(G) $ and a (non-proper) $ k $ -colouring of $ E(G) $ such that edges with the same colour do not cross with respect to $ \preceq $ . That is, if $ v\prec x\prec w\prec y $ for some edges $ vw,xy\in E(G) $ , then $ vw $ and $ xy $ receive distinct colours. One can think that the vertices are placed along the spine of a book, and the edges are drawn without crossings on the pages of the book. The book thickness of $ G $ , denoted by bt $ (G) $ is the minimum integer $ k $ for which there is a $ k $ -page book embedding of $ G $ . Let $ G' $ be the graph obtained by subdividing each edge of $ G $ exactly once. Conjecture There is a function $ f $ such that for every graph $ G $ , $$ \text{bt}(G) \leq f( \text{bt}(G') )\enspace. $$

source_propositions.text: There is a function $ f $ such that for every graph $ G $ , $$ \text{bt}(G) \leq f( \text{bt}(G') )\enspace. $$

rows.rocq_idiom: exists f, forall G, book_thickness G <= f (book_thickness (subdivide1 G))

rows.title: Circular flow numbers of r-graphs
rows.source_text: A nowhere-zero $ r $ -flow $ (D(G),\phi) $ on $ G $ is an orientation $ D $ of $ G $ together with a function $ \phi $ from the edge set of $ G $ into the real numbers such that $ 1 \leq |\phi(e)| \leq r-1 $ , for all $ e \in E(G) $ , and $ \sum_{e \in E^+(v)}\phi(e) = \sum_{e \in E^-(v)}\phi(e), \textrm{ for all } v \in V(G) $ . A $ (2t+1) $ -regular graph $ G $ is a $ (2t+1) $ -graph if $ |\partial_G(X)| \geq 2t+1 $ for every $ X \subseteq V(G) $ with $ |X| $ odd. Conjecture Let $ t > 1 $ be an integer. If $ G $ is a $ (2t+1) $ -graph, then $ F_c(G) \leq 2 + \frac{2}{t} $ .

source_propositions.text: Let $ t > 1 $ be an integer. If $ G $ is a $ (2t+1) $ -graph, then $ F_c(G) \leq 2 + \frac{2}{t} $ .

source_propositions.text: Let $ G $ be a $ 2 $ -connected cubic graph and let $ S $ be a $ 2 $ -regular subgraph such that $ G-E(S) $ is connected. Then $ G $ has a cycle double cover which contains $ S $ (i.e all cycles of $ S $ ).

rows.title: Strong edge colouring conjecture
rows.source_text: A strong edge-colouring of a graph $ G $ is a edge-colouring in which every colour class is an induced matching; that is, any two vertices belonging to distinct edges with the same colour are not adjacent. The strong chromatic index $ s\chi'(G) $ is the minimum number of colours in a strong edge-colouring of $ G $ . Conjecture $$s\chi'(G) \leq \frac{5\Delta^2}{4}, \text{if $\Delta$ is even,}$$ $$s\chi'(G) \leq \frac{5\Delta^2-2\Delta +1}{4},&\text{if $\Delta$ is odd.}$$

source_propositions.text: $$s\chi'(G) \leq \frac{5\Delta^2}{4}, \text{if $\Delta$ is even,}$$ $$s\chi'(G) \leq \frac{5\Delta^2-2\Delta +1}{4},&\text{if $\Delta$ is odd.}$$

rows.title: Strong matchings and covers
rows.source_text: Let $ H $ be a hypergraph . A strongly maximal matching is a matching $ F \subseteq E(H) $ so that $ |F' \setminus F| \le |F \setminus F'| $ for every matching $ F' $ . A strongly minimal cover is a (vertex) cover $ X \subseteq V(H) $ so that $ |X' \setminus X| \ge |X \setminus X'| $ for every cover $ X' $ . Conjecture If $ H $ is a (possibly infinite) hypergraph in which all edges have size $ \le k $ for some integer $ k $ , then $ H $ has a strongly maximal matching and a strongly minimal cover.

source_propositions.text: If $ H $ is a (possibly infinite) hypergraph in which all edges have size $ \le k $ for some integer $ k $ , then $ H $ has a strongly maximal matching and a strongly minimal cover.

rows.source_text: We are given a complete simple undirected weighted graph $ G_1=(V,E) $ and its first arbitrary shortest spanning tree $ T_1=(V,E_1) $ . We define the next graph $ G_2=(V,E\setminus E_1) $ and find on $ G_2 $ the second arbitrary shortest spanning tree $ T_2=(V,E_2) $ . We continue similarly by finding $ T_3=(V,E_3) $ on $ G_3=(V,E\setminus \cup_{i=1}^{2}E_i) $ , etc. Let k be the smallest number of disjoint shortest spanning trees as defined above and let $ T^{k}=(V,\cup_{i=1}^{k}E_i) $ be the graph obtained as union of all $ k $ disjoint trees. Question 1 . What is the smallest number of disjoint spanning trees creates a graph $ T^{k} $ containing a Hamiltonian path. Question 2 . What is the smallest number of disjoint spanning trees creates a graph $ T^{k} $ containing a shortest Hamiltonian path? Questions 3 and 4 . Replace in questions 1 and 2 a shortest spanning tree by a 1-tree. What is the smallest number of disjoint 1-trees creates a Hamiltonian graph? What is the smallest number of disjoint 1-trees creates a graph containing a shortest Hamiltonian cycle?

rows.title: Monochromatic reachability or rainbow triangles
rows.source_text: In an edge-colored digraph, we say that a subgraph is rainbow if all its edges have distinct colors, and monochromatic if all its edges have the same color. Let $ r(k,n) $ be the smallest integer $ r\ge 2 $ such that the graph $ (\text{SE}(k,n))^{r-1} $ is rearrangeable. Problem Find $ r(k,n) $ . Conjecture $ r(k,n)=2n-1 $ .

rows.title: Unfriendly partitions
rows.source_text: If $ G $ is a graph, we say that a partition of $ V(G) $ is unfriendly if every vertex has at least as many neighbors in the other classes as in its own. Problem Does every countably infinite graph have an unfriendly partition into two sets?

source_propositions.text: Does every countably infinite graph have an unfriendly partition into two sets?

rows.rocq_idiom: every countably infinite graph has a 2-partition where each vertex has >= as many cross neighbours as own-class

rows.title: Arc-disjoint directed cycles in regular directed graphs

rows.title: Choice Number of k-Chromatic Graphs of Bounded Order

rows.rocq_idiom: forall G m, chi G = k -> #|V G| <= m*k -> ch G <= ch (Kmk m k)

rows.title: Complete bipartite subgraphs of perfect graphs

source_propositions.text: Let $ G $ be a perfect graph on $ n $ vertices. Is it true that either $ G $ or $ \bar{G} $ contains a complete bipartite subgraph with bipartition $ (A,B) $ so that $ |A|, |B| \ge n^{1 - o(1)} $ ?

rows.rocq_idiom: forall G, perfect G -> exists A B, Kbip in G or compl /\ |A|,|B| >= n^{1-o(1)}

rows.title: Decomposing an even tournament in directed paths

rows.title: End-Devouring Rays

Prove that there is a set $ K' $ of pairwise disjoint $ \omega $ -rays that devours $ \omega $ such that the set of starting vertices of rays in $ K' $ equals the set of starting vertices of rays in $ K $ .

rows.title: Good Edge Labelings
rows.source_text: Question What is the maximum edge density of a graph which has a good edge labeling? We say that a graph is good-edge-labeling critical , if it has no good edge labeling, but every proper subgraph has a good edge labeling. Conjecture For every $ c<4 $ , there is only a finite number of good-edge-labeling critical graphs with average degree less than $ c $ .

source_propositions.text: What is the maximum edge density of a graph which has a good edge labeling?
For every $ c<4 $ , there is only a finite number of good-edge-labeling critical graphs with average degree less than $ c $ .

rows.rocq_idiom: finiteness of critical graphs with average degree < c; edge-density max

rows.title: Hoàng-Reed Conjecture
rows.source_text: Conjecture Every digraph in which each vertex has outdegree at least $ k $ contains $ k $ directed cycles $ C_1, \ldots, C_k $ such that $ C_j $ meets $ \cup_{i=1}^{j-1}C_i $ in at most one vertex, $ 2 \leq j \leq k $ .

source_propositions.text: Every digraph in which each vertex has outdegree at least $ k $ contains $ k $ directed cycles $ C_1, \ldots, C_k $ such that $ C_j $ meets $ \cup_{i=1}^{j-1}C_i $ in at most one vertex, $ 2 \leq j \leq k $ .

rows.status_semantics: PARTIAL — proven for k ≤ 3; general k open. Already formalized in digraph-theory (hoang_reed_statement).
rows.rocq_idiom: min outdeg >= k -> exists C_1..C_k dicycles, each C_j meets union of earlier in <=1 vertex

rows.title: Nearly spanning regular subgraphs
rows.source_text: Conjecture For every $ \epsilon > 0 $ and every positive integer $ k $ , there exists $ r_0 = r_0(\epsilon,k) $ so that every simple $ r $ -regular graph $ G $ with $ r \ge r_0 $ has a $ k $ -regular subgraph $ H $ with $ |V(H)| \ge (1- \epsilon) |V(G)| $ .

source_propositions.text: For every $ \epsilon > 0 $ and every positive integer $ k $ , there exists $ r_0 = r_0(\epsilon,k) $ so that every simple $ r $ -regular graph $ G $ with $ r \ge r_0 $ has a $ k $ -regular subgraph $ H $ with $ |V(H)| \ge (1- \epsilon) |V(G)| $ .

rows.rocq_idiom: forall eps k, exists r0, forall G r>=r0, regular -> exists H k-regular subgraph, |V H| >= (1-eps)|V G|

rows.title: Pebbling a cartesian product
rows.source_text: We let $ p(G) $ denote the pebbling number of a graph $ G $ . Conjecture $ p(G_1 \Box G_2) \le p(G_1) p(G_2) $ .
rows.rocq_idiom: forall G1 G2, p (G1 box G2) <= p G1 * p G2

rows.title: Sidorenko's Conjecture
rows.source_text: Conjecture For any bipartite graph $ H $ and graph $ G $ , the number of homomorphisms from $ H $ to $ G $ is at least $ \left(\frac{2|E(G)|}{|V(G)|^2}\right)^{|E(H)|}|V(G)|^{|V(H)|} $ .

source_propositions.text: For any bipartite graph $ H $ and graph $ G $ , the number of homomorphisms from $ H $ to $ G $ is at least $ \left(\frac{2|E(G)|}{|V(G)|^2}\right)^{|E(H)|}|V(G)|^{|V(H)|} $ .

rows.rocq_idiom: forall H bipartite, G, hom(H,G) >= (2|E G|/|V G|^2)^|E H| |V G|^|V H|

rows.title: Complexity of the H-factor problem.
rows.source_text: An $ H $ -factor in a graph $ G $ is a set of vertex-disjoint copies of $ H $ covering all vertices of $ G $ . Problem Let $ c $ be a fixed positive real number and $ H $ a fixed graph. Is it NP-hard to determine whether a graph $ G $ on $ n $ vertices and minimum degree $ cn $ contains and $ H $ -factor?

source_propositions.text: Let $ c $ be a fixed positive real number and $ H $ a fixed graph. Is it NP-hard to determine whether a graph $ G $ on $ n $ vertices and minimum degree $ cn $ contains and $ H $ -factor?

rows.source_text: Conjecture If $ G $ is a simple graph which is the union of $ k $ pairwise edge-disjoint complete graphs, each of which has $ k $ vertices, then the chromatic number of $ G $ is $ k $ .

source_propositions.text: If $ G $ is a simple graph which is the union of $ k $ pairwise edge-disjoint complete graphs, each of which has $ k $ vertices, then the chromatic number of $ G $ is $ k $ .

rows.title: Signing a graph to have small magnitude eigenvalues
rows.source_text: Conjecture If $ A $ is the adjacency matrix of a $ d $ -regular graph, then there is a symmetric signing of $ A $ (i.e. replace some $ +1 $ entries by $ -1 $ ) so that the resulting matrix has all eigenvalues of magnitude at most $ 2 \sqrt{d-1} $ .

source_propositions.text: If $ A $ is the adjacency matrix of a $ d $ -regular graph, then there is a symmetric signing of $ A $ (i.e. replace some $ +1 $ entries by $ -1 $ ) so that the resulting matrix has all eigenvalues of magnitude at most $ 2 \sqrt{d-1} $ .

rows.status_semantics: SOLVED (Bilu–Linial signing → bipartite Ramanujan, Marcus–Spielman–Srivastava 2015). DEFERRED (spectral, D5); stated as a Definition only.

rows.title: Erdős-Posa property for long directed cycles
rows.source_text: Conjecture Let $ \ell \geq 2 $ be an integer. For every integer $ n\geq 0 $ , there exists an integer $ t_n=t_n(\ell) $ such that for every digraph $ D $ , either $ D $ has a $ n $ pairwise-disjoint directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ , or there exists a set $ T $ of at most $ t_n $ vertices such that $ D-T $ has no directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ .

source_propositions.text: Let $ \ell \geq 2 $ be an integer. For every integer $ n\geq 0 $ , there exists an integer $ t_n=t_n(\ell) $ such that for every digraph $ D $ , either $ D $ has a $ n $ pairwise-disjoint directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ , or there exists a set $ T $ of at most $ t_n $ vertices such that $ D-T $ has no directed cycles of length at least $ \ell $ .

rows.status_semantics: PARTIAL — the (unrestricted) directed Erdős–Pósa property is known (Reed–Robertson–Seymour–Thomas); the LONG-directed-cycle variant is open. Already formalized (erdos_posa_long_dicycles_statement).
rows.rocq_idiom: forall l n, exists t, forall D, (exists n disjoint dicycles len>=l) \/ (exists T, #|T|<=t /\ D-T has no dicycle len>=l)

source_propositions.text: If $ G $ is a simple digraph without directed cycles of length $ \le 3 $ , then $ \beta(G) \le \frac{1}{2} \gamma(G) $ .

rows.title: Vertex Coloring of graph fractional powers
rows.source_text: Conjecture Let $ G $ be a graph and $ k $ be a positive integer. The $ k- $ power of $ G $ , denoted by $ G^k $ , is defined on the vertex set $ V(G) $ , by connecting any two distinct vertices $ x $ and $ y $ with distance at most $ k $ . In other words, $ E(G^k)=\{xy:1\leq d_G(x,y)\leq k\} $ . Also $ k- $ subdivision of $ G $ , denoted by $ G^\frac{1}{k} $ , is constructed by replacing each edge $ ij $ of $ G $ with a path of length $ k $ . Note that for $ k=1 $ , we have $ G^\frac{1}{1}=G^1=G $ . Now we can define the fractional power of a graph as follows: Let $ G $ be a graph and $ m,n\in \mathbb{N} $ . The graph $ G^{\frac{m}{n}} $ is defined by the $ m- $ power of the $ n- $ subdivision of $ G $ . In other words $ G^{\frac{m}{n}}\isdef (G^{\frac{1}{n}})^m $ . Conjecture. Let $ G $ be a connected graph with $ \Delta(G)\geq3 $ and $ m $ be a positive integer greater than 1. Then for any positive integer $ n>m $ , we have $ \chi(G^{\frac{m}{n}})=\omega(G^\frac{m}{n}) $ . In [1], it was shown that this conjecture is true in some special cases.

rows.title: Circular choosability of planar graphs
rows.source_text: Let $ G = (V, E) $ be a graph. If $ p $ and $ q $ are two integers, a $ (p,q) $ -colouring of $ G $ is a function $ c $ from $ V $ to $ \{0,\dots,p-1\} $ such that $ q \le |c(u)-c(v)| \le p-q $ for each edge $ uv\in E $ . Given a list assignment $ L $ of $ G $ , i.e.~a mapping that assigns to every vertex $ v $ a set of non-negative integers, an $ L $ -colouring of $ G $ is a mapping $ c : V \to N $ such that $ c(v)\in L(v) $ for every $ v\in V $ . A list assignment $ L $ is a $ t $ - $ (p,q) $ -list-assignment if $ L(v) \subseteq \{0,\dots,p-1\} $ and $ |L(v)| \ge tq $ for each vertex $ v \in V $ . Given such a list assignment $ L $ , the graph G is $ (p,q) $ - $ L $ -colourable if there exists a $ (p,q) $ - $ L $ -colouring $ c $ , i.e. $ c $ is both a $ (p,q) $ -colouring and an $ L $ -colouring. For any real number $ t \ge 1 $ , the graph $ G $ is $ t $ - $ (p,q) $ -choosable if it is $ (p,q) $ - $ L $ -colourable for every $ t $ - $ (p,q) $ -list-assignment $ L $ . Last, $ G $ is circularly $ t $ -choosable if it is $ t $ - $ (p,q) $ -choosable for any $ p $ , $ q $ . The circular choosability (or circular list chromatic number or circular choice number) of G is $$cch(G) := \inf\{t \ge 1 : G \text{ is circularly $t$-choosable}\}.$$ Problem What is the best upper bound on circular choosability for planar graphs?

rows.title: Total Colouring Conjecture
rows.source_text: Conjecture A total coloring of a graph $ G = (V,E) $ is an assignment of colors to the vertices and the edges of $ G $ such that every pair of adjacent vertices, every pair of adjacent edges and every vertex and incident edge pair, receive different colors. The total chromatic number of a graph $ G $ , $ \chi''(G) $ , equals the minimum number of colors needed in a total coloring of $ G $ . It is an old conjecture of Behzad that for every graph $ G $ , the total chromatic number equals the maximum degree of a vertex in $ G $ , $ \Delta(G) $ plus one or two. In other words, \[\chi''(G)=\Delta(G)+1\ \ or \ \ \Delta(G)+2.\]

rows.title: Circular flow number of regular class 1 graphs
rows.source_text: A nowhere-zero $ r $ -flow $ (D(G),\phi) $ on $ G $ is an orientation $ D $ of $ G $ together with a function $ \phi $ from the edge set of $ G $ into the real numbers such that $ 1 \leq |\phi(e)| \leq r-1 $ , for all $ e \in E(G) $ , and $ \sum_{e \in E^+(v)}\phi(e) = \sum_{e \in E^-(v)}\phi(e), \textrm{ for all } v \in V(G) $ . The circular flow number of $ G $ is inf $ \{ r | G $ has a nowhere-zero $ r $ -flow $ \} $ , and it is denoted by $ F_c(G) $ . A graph with maximum vertex degree $ k $ is a class 1 graph if its edge chromatic number is $ k $ . Conjecture Let $ t \geq 1 $ be an integer and $ G $ a $ (2t+1) $ -regular graph. If $ G $ is a class 1 graph, then $ F_c(G) \leq 2 + \frac{2}{t} $ .

rows.title: Ryser's conjecture
rows.source_text: Conjecture Let $ H $ be an $ r $ - uniform $ r $ -partite hypergraph. If $ \nu $ is the maximum number of pairwise disjoint edges in $ H $ , and $ \tau $ is the size of the smallest set of vertices which meets every edge, then $ \tau \le (r-1) \nu $ .

source_propositions.text: Let $ H $ be an $ r $ - uniform $ r $ -partite hypergraph. If $ \nu $ is the maximum number of pairwise disjoint edges in $ H $ , and $ \tau $ is the size of the smallest set of vertices which meets every edge, then $ \tau \le (r-1) \nu $ .

rows.rocq_idiom: forall H r-uniform r-partite, tau H <= (r-1) * nu H

rows.title: Strong colorability
rows.source_text: Let $ r $ be a positive integer. We say that a graph $ G $ is strongly $ r $ -colorable if for every partition of the vertices to sets of size at most $ r $ there is a proper $ r $ -coloring of $ G $ in which the vertices in each set of the partition have distinct colors. Conjecture If $ \Delta $ is the maximal degree of a graph $ G $ , then $ G $ is strongly $ 2 \Delta $ -colorable.

source_propositions.text: If $ \Delta $ is the maximal degree of a graph $ G $ , then $ G $ is strongly $ 2 \Delta $ -colorable.

rows.status_semantics: PARTIAL — strong chromatic number bounds (e.g. ≤ 3Δ−1) are known; the conjectured 2Δ is open.
rows.rocq_idiom: forall G, strongly_colorable G (2 * maxdeg G)

rows.title: The three 4-flows conjecture
rows.source_text: Conjecture For every graph $ G $ with no bridge , there exist three disjoint sets $ A_1,A_2,A_3 \subseteq E(G) $ with $ A_1 \cup A_2 \cup A_3 = E(G) $ so that $ G \setminus A_i $ has a nowhere-zero 4-flow for $ 1 \le i \le 3 $ .

source_propositions.text: For every graph $ G $ with no bridge , there exist three disjoint sets $ A_1,A_2,A_3 \subseteq E(G) $ with $ A_1 \cup A_2 \cup A_3 = E(G) $ so that $ G \setminus A_i $ has a nowhere-zero 4-flow for $ 1 \le i \le 3 $ .

rows.rocq_idiom: forall G bridgeless, exists A1 A2 A3 partition E, forall i, nz4flow (G minus Ai)

rows.title: Weighted colouring of hexagonal graphs
rows.source_text: Conjecture There is an absolute constant $ c $ such that for every hexagonal graph $ G $ and vertex weighting $ p:V(G)\rightarrow \mathbb{N} $ , $$\chi(G,p) \leq \frac{9}{8}\omega(G,p) + c $$

source_propositions.text: There is an absolute constant $ c $ such that for every hexagonal graph $ G $ and vertex weighting $ p:V(G)\rightarrow \mathbb{N} $ , $$\chi(G,p) \leq \frac{9}{8}\omega(G,p) + c $$

rows.rocq_idiom: exists c, forall hexagonal G, weighting p, chi(G,p) <= (9/8) omega(G,p) + c
